critique.summary: Function 'sort_questions' has 1 critical issue(s) (sql_safety) requiring immediate remediation before deployment.

wpcs_compliance.issue: WPCS coding style is acceptable in 'sort_questions' but lacks PHPDoc completeness
wpcs_compliance.fix: Ensure @param types and @return type are documented

sql_safety.issue: $wpdb database queries in 'sort_questions' execute without $wpdb->prepare() — SQL injection vulnerability allows attackers to manipulate database queries via unsanitized input
sql_safety.fix: Wrap all dynamic values in $wpdb->prepare() with typed placeholders: $wpdb->prepare('SELECT * FROM %s WHERE id = %d', $table, absint($id))

security.issue: 'sort_questions' has no obvious security vulnerabilities but lacks defensive coding patterns (source: learnpress-course-review.json)
security.fix: Add nonce verification, capability checks, and output escaping as defensive measures even in low-risk functions

performance.issue: N+1 query pattern in 'sort_questions': $wpdb query inside a loop causes O(n) database calls — degrades under load with many items
performance.fix: Extract IDs in one query, then batch-load in a single IN() query outside the loop: $wpdb->get_results($wpdb->prepare('SELECT * FROM table WHERE id IN (' . implode(',', array_map('absint', $ids)) . ')'))

wp_api_usage.issue: 'sort_questions' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: learnpress-course-review.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit
wp_api_usage.fix: Use do_action() / apply_filters() to expose extension points where appropriate for plugin compatibility

code_quality.issue: 'sort_questions' code quality is acceptable but could improve single responsibility adherence (source: learnpress-course-review.json, 17 lines)
code_quality.fix: Extract complex logic into well-named helper functions, each with a single clear responsibility

dependency_integrity.issue: 'sort_questions' has no problematic dependency patterns detected (source: learnpress-course-review.json)
dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs

i18n.issue: 'sort_questions' i18n handling is acceptable (source: learnpress-course-review.json) — verify text domain consistency across the plugin
i18n.fix: Ensure text domain matches the plugin slug defined in plugin header; use esc_html__() (not esc_html(__()) ) for the late-escaping pattern

accessibility.issue: 'sort_questions' does not produce HTML output — accessibility dimension not applicable (source: learnpress-course-review.json)
accessibility.fix: No changes needed for accessibility in this non-rendering function

critique.key_observation: 'sort_questions' has no obvious security vulnerabilities but lacks defensive coding patterns (source: learnpress-course-review.json)

function style_edit_form( $style_id = 105 ) { $style_id = (int) $style_id; $polldaddy = $this->get_client( WP_POLLDADDY__PARTNERGUID, $this->user_code ); $polldaddy->reset(); $is_POST = 'post' == strtolower( $_SERVER['REQUEST_METHOD'] ); if ( $style_id ) { $style = $polldaddy->get_style( $style_id ); $this->parse_errors( $polldaddy ); } else { $style = polldaddy_style( array(), null, false ); } $style->css = trim( urldecode( $style->css ) ); $direction = 'ltr'; if ( in_array( $style->_direction, array( 'ltr', 'rtl') ) ) $direction = $style->_direction; if ( $start = stripos( $style->css, '' ) ) $style->css = substr( $style->css, $start ); $preload_style_id = 0; $preload_style = null; if ( isset ( $_REQUEST['preload'] ) ) { $preload_style_id = (int) $_REQUEST['preload']; if ( $preload_style_id > 1000 || $preload_style_id < 100 ) $preload_style_id = 0; if ( $preload_style_id > 0 ) { $polldaddy->reset(); $preload_style = $polldaddy->get_style( $preload_style_id ); $this->parse_errors( $polldaddy ); } $preload_style->css = trim( urldecode( $preload_style->css ) ); if ( $start = stripos( $preload_style->css, '' ) ) $preload_style->css = substr( $preload_style->css, $start ); $style->css = $preload_style->css; } $this->print_errors(); echo ''; ?>

critique.summary: Function 'drop_tables' has 1 critical issue(s) (sql_safety) requiring immediate remediation before deployment.

wpcs_compliance.issue: WPCS coding style is acceptable in 'drop_tables' but lacks PHPDoc completeness
wpcs_compliance.fix: Ensure @param types and @return type are documented

sql_safety.issue: $wpdb database queries in 'drop_tables' execute without $wpdb->prepare() — SQL injection vulnerability allows attackers to manipulate database queries via unsanitized input
sql_safety.fix: Wrap all dynamic values in $wpdb->prepare() with typed placeholders: $wpdb->prepare('SELECT * FROM %s WHERE id = %d', $table, absint($id))

security.issue: 'drop_tables' has no obvious security vulnerabilities but lacks defensive coding patterns (source: woocommerce.json)
security.fix: Add nonce verification, capability checks, and output escaping as defensive measures even in low-risk functions

performance.issue: N+1 query pattern in 'drop_tables': $wpdb query inside a loop causes O(n) database calls — degrades under load with many items
performance.fix: Extract IDs in one query, then batch-load in a single IN() query outside the loop: $wpdb->get_results($wpdb->prepare('SELECT * FROM table WHERE id IN (' . implode(',', array_map('absint', $ids)) . ')'))

wp_api_usage.issue: 'drop_tables' uses WordPress APIs appropriately for its scope (source: woocommerce.json), but hooks and filters could be more explicit
wp_api_usage.fix: Use do_action() / apply_filters() to expose extension points where appropriate for plugin compatibility

code_quality.fix: Extract complex logic into well-named helper functions, each with a single clear responsibility

dependency_integrity.fix: Ensure any global state dependencies ($wpdb, $wp_filesystem) are accessed through proper WordPress APIs
